intro: Converting code from JavaScript to PHP swaps a front-to-back asynchronous runtime for a server-side engine traditionally optimized for synchronous HTTP request-response processing. Developers perform this transition when working with classic content management architectures or consolidating specialized backend web services. The migration model moves from an ongoing single-threaded loop process to an isolated, request-scoped execution lifecycle.

diffs.from: Dynamic types evaluated at runtime with automatic coercion rules and prototypal object linking.
diffs.to: Gradual typing system supporting strong explicit scalar type declarations and strict mode settings per file.

diffs.from: Modern scripting syntax using arrow notations, implicit variable declarations, and camelCase conventions.
diffs.to: Sigil-based syntax requiring dollar signs ($) for variables, explicit semicolons, and arrow mapping operators (->).

diffs.from: Persistent engine-level allocations tracking variables throughout long-running application process loops.
diffs.to: Short-lived stateless application memory footprint that is completely allocated and purged on every individual HTTP request loop.

diffs.from: Native asynchronous processing model utilizing internal event queues, promises, and non-blocking tasks.
diffs.to: Synchronous sequential execution loops; asynchronous tasks require external workers or task-runner queues.

diffs.from: Built around web browser interfaces, text parsing APIs, and Node ecosystem modules.
diffs.to: Extensive built-in array operations, SQL connectivity, and server-side web utility modules.

examples.title: Associative Array Mapping
examples.description: Translating structural JavaScript object properties into functional PHP associative array syntax.

examples.title: Class Structuring
examples.description: Converting public JavaScript constructors into modern PHP typed properties and instance methods.
examples.toCode:
<?php
class Logger {
    private string $prefix;
    public function __construct(string $prefix) {
        $this->prefix = $prefix;
    }
    public function log(string $msg): void {
        echo "{$this->prefix}: {$msg}";
    }
}

tips.title: Prepend Variables with Sigils
tips.body: Remember to add the mandatory '$' prefix character to all variables when translating logic blocks.

tips.title: Activate Strict Types
tips.body: Enforce strong typing discipline by declaring 'declare(strict_types=1);' at the absolute top of every converted PHP file.

tips.title: Swap String Concatenations
tips.body: Change your string joining syntax symbols from plus signs ('+') over to standard PHP dot ('.') operator tokens.

tips.title: Rethink Persistent Caching
tips.body: Do not store runtime parameters in simple global arrays; since memory clears after every request, utilize persistent layer stores like Redis.

faqs.question: What replaces JavaScript Array.length in PHP?
faqs.answer: Use the built-in count($array) function to evaluate collection lengths dynamically.

faqs.question: How do I handle asynchronous code blocks in PHP?
faqs.answer: Standard PHP is strictly synchronous. To run background jobs, dispatch tasks out to system message queues like RabbitMQ or use async runtimes like Swoole.

faqs.question: Are PHP objects passed by reference?
faqs.answer: Yes. In modern PHP, object variables act as data references, mirroring how JavaScript objects operate when passed into functions.
